seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with CrewAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with CrewAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with CrewAI enables multi-agent collaboration for intent detection, draft generation, attachment handling, and approval workflows, streamlining email processing at scale. However, agent delegation loops, hallucinated outputs, and task drift can lead to sending inappropriate or inaccurate emails. Vex guardrails ensure reliability by verifying PII filtering, tone, recipient validity, content accuracy, and confidentiality before actions execute.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with CrewAI
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant uses CrewAI to orchestrate four specialized agents: Intent Detector classifies incoming email purpose (reply, forward, draft); Draft Generator creates contextually appropriate responses; Attachment Handler extracts, validates, and manages files; Approval Agent reviews for compliance before sending. Agents collaborate sequentially with shared context, passing email metadata, content, and decisions between tasks.

Each agent leverages CrewAI's task delegation with tools for email APIs (Gmail/IMAP), PII detection, and tone analysis. The crew manager coordinates execution, but without guardrails, agents may misinterpret instructions, loop indefinitely, or generate unsafe content. Vex integrates as task callbacks to verify outputs at each handoff.

This architecture handles high-volume email (1000+/day) but demands strict verification due to confidentiality risks and recipient impact.

risks.name: Agent delegation loops
risks.description: Intent Detector endlessly re-queries Draft Generator for clarification, stalling email processing
risks.mitigation: Vex task callbacks enforce max iterations and detect circular delegation patterns

risks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
risks.description: Attachment Handler fabricates file paths or PII data passed to Approval Agent
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs against email content and filesystem before agent handoff

risks.name: Task drift in tone control
risks.description: Draft Generator shifts from professional to casual tone across iterations
risks.mitigation: Vex sentiment analysis ensures tone consistency with original email context

risks.name: PII leakage in drafts
risks.description: Agents include unredacted personal data in response drafts
risks.mitigation: Vex PII scanner blocks drafts containing SSNs, emails, or phone numbers

implementation.title: CrewAI Agents Setup
implementation.description: Define the four core agents with specific roles and tools for email processing workflow
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from vex import Vex

intent_detector = Agent(
    role='Email Intent Detector',
    goal='Classify email intent: reply, forward, or archive',
    backstory='Expert at understanding email communication patterns',
    verbose=True
)

draft_generator = Agent(
    role='Email Draft Writer',
    goal='Generate professional email responses matching detected intent',
    backstory='Senior technical writer specializing in business communication',
    verbose=True
)

attachment_handler = Agent(
    role='Attachment Processor',
    goal='Extract and validate email attachments for security',
    backstory='Cybersecurity specialist for file handling',
    verbose=True
)

approval_agent = Agent(
    role='Compliance Reviewer',
    goal='Verify email content meets company policy before sending',
    backstory='Legal compliance officer for corporate communications',
    verbose=True
)

implementation.title: Task Definitions with Vex Callbacks
implementation.description: Each task includes Vex verification before completion to catch failures early
implementation.code:
def vex_callback(task_output):
    vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")
    result = vex.verify(
        input=task.context,
        output=task_output,
        checks=[
            "pii_filtering",
            "tone_appropriateness",
            "recipient_validation"
        ]
    )
    return result.approved

intent_task = Task(
    description='Analyze {email_content} and classify intent',
    agent=intent_detector,
    context={'email_content': '{{inputs.email}}'},
    callback=vex_callback
)

draft_task = Task(
    description='Write response for intent: {intent_classification}',
    agent=draft_generator,
    context={'intent_classification': intent_task.output},
    callback=vex_callback
)

implementation.title: Crew Orchestration
implementation.description: Assemble agents into crew with sequential task execution and shared memory
implementation.code:
email_crew = Crew(
    agents=[intent_detector, draft_generator, attachment_handler, approval_agent],
    tasks=[intent_task, draft_task, attachment_task, approval_task],
    verbose=2,
    memory=True,
    max_rpm=50,
    share_crew=False
)

# Execute with input email
result = email_crew.kickoff(inputs={
    'email': 'Subject: Urgent contract review... Dear John, please review...'
})

print('Final approved email:', result)

implementation.title: Email Sending with Final Vex Check
implementation.description: Approval task integrates with email API only after Vex compliance verification
implementation.code:
def send_approved_email(email_content, recipients):
    final_vex_check = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key").verify(
        input=email_content,
        output={"action": "send", "recipients": recipients},
        checks=[
            "confidentiality_enforcement",
            "content_accuracy",
            "recipient_validation"
        ]
    )
    
    if final_vex_check.approved:
        import smtplib
        server = smtplib.SMTP('smtp.gmail.com', 587)
        server.sendmail('sender@company.com', recipients, email_content)
        server.quit()
        return "Email sent successfully"
    else:
        return f"Blocked: {final_vex_check.issues}"

vexIntegration.title: PII Filtering Callback
vexIntegration.description: Prevents PII leakage by scanning all agent outputs before task completion
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Vex

@vex.guardrail(checks=["pii_filtering", "confidentiality_enforcement"])
def safe_draft_generation(email_context, intent):
    """Generates email draft with automatic PII redaction"""
    # Agent would generate draft here
    draft = f"Response to {intent}: {email_context['body'][:200]}..."
    return draft

# Usage in task
result = safe_draft_generation(
    email_context={"body": "SSN: 123-45-6789, contact john.doe@company.com"},
    intent="reply"
)
# Vex automatically redacts SSN and validates before returning

vexIntegration.title: Tone Consistency Verification
vexIntegration.description: Ensures generated drafts maintain professional tone matching original email

vexIntegration.title: Recipient Validation Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Prevents sending to invalid or external domains without approval

cta.heading: Secure Your CrewAI AI Email Assistant
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and protect your email workflows from agent failures.
